item.title: Biggest US Nuclear Fuel Enricher Is Scaling Up in Bet on AI Boom
item.description: Urenco USA, the only US commercial uranium-enrichment company, is planning a “multi-billion-dollar” investment to boost capacity by almost 50% on expectation of surging demand for nuclear reactors.

item.title: PE-Owned JetBio Plans Landmark Green Jet Fuel Plant in Brazil
item.description: Private equity-controlled JetBio plans to build the world’s biggest commercial green jet fuel plant that uses ethanol in Brazil to take advantage of the country’s ample feedstock alternatives.

item.title: India’s Key Monsoon Rains Set to Arrive Days After Typical Start
item.description: India’s annual monsoon rains that are crucial for farm output are likely to arrive around Thursday at Kerala state on the country’s southern tip, a few days after the official start date.

item.title: Judge Blocks Trump Administration From Breaking Up Climate Research Center
item.description: A judge has temporarily blocked the National Science Foundation from dismantling the National Center for Atmospheric Research, or NCAR, a globally significant weather and climate research center based in Boulder, Colorado.

item.title: Wildfires in Alberta Die Down as Rain Storm Moves Into Region
item.description: Wildfires that erupted on Friday and Saturday in the Canadian oil sands-producing region of Lac la Biche in Alberta have been reclassified as being held or under control, according to Alberta Wildfire.

item.title: California Revamps Its Carbon Market and Gets Tough on Plastic Waste
item.description: Ambitious green policies have been “upended by global economic upheaval,” a state official said.

item.title: EPA Climate Rollback Counts Savings, Downplays Costs
item.description: The agency estimates delaying a super-pollutant phaseout will result in millions of dollars in savings, but its calculation doesn’t take many costs into account.

item.title: El Niño Offers Less Relief Than Hurricane Forecasts Suggest
item.description: El Niño may limit the number of hurricanes this season, but one landfall can still hit insurers, energy, orange juice and power grids.

item.title: California Launches First Phase of Plastic Crackdown Amid Chorus of Complaints
item.description: Companies and environmental advocates alike say the anti-pollution law lacks robust systems to support it.

item.title: Former Meta CTO Raises Clean Tech Fund as AI Reshapes Sector
item.description: Gigascale Capital closed a $250 million fund for early-stage startups.

item.title: The Race to Rethink Data Centers for AI’s Power Surge
item.description: With energy demand already straining infrastructure, AI players are reimagining the data center from the ground up.

item.title: Arini’s Former Risk Chief Joins Credit Suisse Alum’s Debt-Swap Firm
item.description: Arini Capital Management’s first chief risk officer, James Howard, is joining Enosis Capital LLC, an impact credit specialist led by former Credit Suisse executive, Ramzi Issa.

item.title: China’s Solar Industry Looks to The Stars to Solve Earthly Glut
item.description: Putting solar panels in space is one way of offloading your earthly surplus.

item.title: Most EU Nations Lag in Boosting Company EV Sales With Tax Levers
item.description: Two-thirds of European Union member states are failing to provide adequate tax incentives to encourage companies to favor electric vehicles in corporate fleets, according to a study by a clean transport lobby.

item.title: A Rare ‘Super’ El Niño Is Looking More Likely. Here’s What to Expect
item.description: ​​A cyclical weather phenomenon that can drive flooding in one continent and drought in another looks increasingly likely to emerge this year.

item.title: Alberta Wildfires Break Out in Lac la Biche Oil Sands Region
item.description: Wildfires have erupted in Canada’s oil sands-producing region of Alberta with large blazes within 20 kilometers (12.4 miles) of about half a million barrels a day of crude production.

item.title: How Switzerland Is Adpating to Melting Glaciers
item.description: Climate change is leading to the melting of glaciers and the thawing of permafrost worldwide.

item.title: Peugeot Revival Takes Hold on EV Demand Surge in France, Germany
item.description: Stellantis NV’s Peugeot brand is getting a boost from rising electric-vehicle demand, helping the nameplate emerge from quality issues that tarnished its image and drove away customers.

item.title: California Overhauls Carbon Market Over Affordability Concerns
item.description: State makes $4 billion concession to oil industry amid soaring gasoline prices.

item.title: SEC Moves to Scrap Biden-Era Rule on Climate Risk Disclosures
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission wants to formally kill rules it’s now calling a “dramatic overreach” of its own authority that would have required public companies to report greenhouse gas emissions and climate-related risks to their operations.

item.title: Burberry Pushes Off Net Zero Goal by 10 Years
item.description: The company called the step a pragmatic response to market conditions.

item.title: South Africa Eyes Green Bonds For $228 Billion ESG Finance Plan
item.description: South Africa’s Treasury published a framework for sustainable-finance instruments to help raise the 3.7 trillion rand ($228 billion) needed to mitigate the effect of greenhouse—gas emissions over the next decade.

item.title: Paris Heat Wave Set to Peak Before Weekend Relief
item.description: The record-breaking heat wave scorching Europe is forecast to peak Friday in Paris, but relief is on the horizon.

item.title: Indian Power Firms Snap Up Gas to Meet Night-Time Cooling Demand
item.description: India’s power generation companies have quadrupled their natural gas purchases from a domestic bourse over the past couple of months to meet surging power demand from searing summer heat.

item.title: Europe Faces Biggest Food Price Shock in G7 From Hotter World
item.description: A global food crunch due to extreme weather would boost prices more in the euro area than in Group of Seven economies, according to Oxford Economics.

item.title: India Cuts Monsoon Forecast to 90% of Normal in Risk to Crops
item.description: India lowered its forecast for this year’s monsoon, a blow to farmers who are already contending with higher input costs due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Rise of Renewables and EVs in Australia Is Trimming Emissions
item.description: Additions of renewable energy, lower use of fossil fuels and accelerating adoption of electric vehicles cut Australia’s annual greenhouse gas emissions by about 2%, helping to ease concerns the nation — one of the world’s top per-capita polluters — will miss climate targets.

item.title: Americans Doubt Countries Will Do Enough to Tame Climate Change
item.description: Pew Research Center polling finds a majority of US adults view global warming as a significant problem, though with a sharp partisan divide.

item.title: Reform UK Pushes North Sea in Biggest-Yet Energy Firm Meeting
item.description: Reform UK Deputy Leader Richard Tice told energy giants his poll-leading party would focus on cutting bills rather than investing in green energy if it wins the next general election.

item.title: Bezos Earth Fund Lags Behind $10 Billion Spending Goal
item.description: The philanthropy says it’s committed to fulfilling the pledge by 2030.
